questions.text: Explain the difference between a Process and a Thread in Operating Systems. How do they communicate?
questions.idealAnswer: A process is an isolated execution unit with dedicated virtual memory space (inter-process communication needed). A thread is the smallest schedulable execution unit inside a process, sharing memory (heap/code) with other threads.
questions.feedback: No valid answer provided. In a real interview, always attempt to outline your thought process.

questions.text: Given a binary tree, how do you find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two given nodes? What is the complexity?
questions.idealAnswer: Recursively traverse nodes: if node is null, p, or q, return node. Search left and right subtrees. If both subtrees return non-null, current node is LCA. Time complexity is O(N) where N is number of nodes. Space complexity is O(H) recursion stack.
questions.feedback: No valid answer provided. In a real interview, always attempt to outline your thought process.

questions.text: What is the difference between TCP and UDP? Explain the three-way handshake in TCP.
questions.idealAnswer: TCP is connection-oriented, reliable, guarantees packet ordering, and handles congestion. UDP is connectionless, fast, unreliable, and suitable for streaming. TCP handshake: SYN -> SYN-ACK -> ACK.
questions.feedback: No valid answer provided. In a real interview, always attempt to outline your thought process.

questions.text: Explain the difference between normalization and denormalization in DBMS. When would you use denormalization?
questions.idealAnswer: Normalization structures tables to eliminate redundancy and improve integrity (1NF, 2NF, 3NF). Denormalization intentionally adds redundant data to speed up complex query executions in read-heavy/analytical workloads.
questions.feedback: No valid answer provided. In a real interview, always attempt to outline your thought process.

questions.text: How would you design a rate limiter for a public API? What algorithms (like Token Bucket) would you apply?
questions.idealAnswer: Protects APIs from abuse. Algorithms include Token Bucket (allows bursts), Leaky Bucket (smooths rates), Fixed Window, and Sliding Window. Can be implemented using Redis to count client IP hits within a sliding timeframe.
questions.feedback: No valid answer provided. In a real interview, always attempt to outline your thought process.
